entries.name: NotNormalized

group.name: Transaction Malformed Errors (110-139, 166-192)
group.description: Transactions that are structurally invalid or fail proof verification.

entries.description: The transaction is not in normal form. The most common cause on a local devnet is a zero fee: when feesWithMargin evaluates to 0 and no additionalFeeOverhead is set, the wallet builds an empty DustActions, which DustActions::well_formed rejects. A ContractDeploy is accepted but the first contract call fails; there is no special minimum-cost floor on ContractDeploy.
fixes: Set a small positive additionalFeeOverhead (e.g. 1_000_000n) in the wallet's costParameters so a real DUST fee is spent
Any positive amount the wallet can cover in DUST works (the basic-start tutorial and compact-cli-dev template use 300_000_000_000_000n)
Build the transaction with the SDK so it is normalized

entries.name: BalanceCheckOverspend

group.name: Transaction Malformed Errors (110-139, 166-192)
group.description: Transactions that are structurally invalid or fail proof verification.

entries.description: A token balance went negative after fees were applied (balancing_check, midnight-ledger ledger/src/verify.rs). The transaction or one of its segments spends more of a token than it has once fees are applied. Because fees are paid in DUST, a DUST-side 138 means the DUST fee exceeded available DUST — not a NIGHT funding-level problem. DUST registration does NOT cause this: registration is self-funding (its fee is paid by the DUST the registered NIGHT UTXOs generate, not the wallet's existing DUST balance), so it succeeds even at a 0 DUST balance — verified on a local devnet, including with additionalFeeOverhead set.
fixes: Reduce the transaction outputs or add more inputs of the overspent token
For a DUST-side overspend, ensure the wallet holds enough DUST for the fee
Do not raise NIGHT funding to fix this — fees are denominated in DUST, not NIGHT

If you intended to reference a value, check that the name resolves to a binding rather than a type or generic parameter

entries.name: Disconnected
entries.description: The connection to the wallet was lost mid-session (e.g. the WebSocket or communication channel dropped). Distinct from a never-connected state.
fixes: Re-establish connection by calling connect(networkId)
Use getConnectionStatus() to observe connection state

entries.name: InternalError
entries.description: An internal error occurred within the dapp connector.
fixes: Check dapp connector logs for details and retry the operation

entries.name: InvalidRequest
entries.description: The DApp sent an invalid or malformed request. Per the spec, this can be thrown in various circumstances — a malformed transaction is one example.
fixes: Review the dapp connector API specification and correct the request format

entries.name: PermissionRejected
entries.description: Session-level / persistent denial — the user has set a general preference to not permit this action for this DApp. Once observed for a particular API method, expect it to keep being returned for the session (until the browser tab/window is closed).
fixes: Inform the user that the DApp is blocked at the wallet-permission level
Direct the user to unblock the DApp in wallet settings if they want to proceed
Do NOT auto-retry — the rejection is persistent for the session

entries.name: Rejected
entries.description: One-time user rejection — the user saw the request (e.g. saw the cost of a transaction) and explicitly declined it.
fixes: Inform the user the action was cancelled
Do not auto-retry; let the user re-initiate

entries.name: JobQueueFull
entries.description: The proof server job queue is full and cannot accept new requests.
fixes: Retry after a short delay
Consider reducing request concurrency

entries.name: JobMissing
entries.description: The requested proof job was not found on the server.
fixes: Verify the job ID is correct

entries.name: JobNotPending
entries.description: The job cannot be processed because it is not in a pending state.
fixes: Check the job status before attempting to process it

entries.name: BadInput
entries.description: The proof server received invalid input for the proof job.
fixes: Verify the circuit inputs are correctly encoded and within valid ranges

entries.name: InternalError
entries.description: An internal error occurred in the proof server.
fixes: Check proof server logs for details
Retry the request

entries.name: ChannelClosed
entries.description: An internal worker channel was unexpectedly closed in the proof server.
fixes: Restart the proof server
File a bug report if the issue persists

entries.name: Busy
entries.description: The proof server is too busy to accept new requests.
fixes: Retry after backing off
Scale the proof server if load is consistently high

entries.name: Ready
entries.description: The indexer is running and ready to serve requests.

entries.name: BadRequest
entries.description: Returned by async-graphql-axum's GraphQLRequest extractor when the request body cannot be parsed into a GraphQL request. Triggers: malformed JSON body (InvalidRequest), JSON-array batch sent to the single-request endpoint (UnsupportedBatch), body-stream I/O failure (Io), or — if Content-Type is multipart/form-data — multipart parse failures (InvalidMultipart, MissingOperatorsPart, MissingMapPart, MissingFiles, NotUpload, InvalidFilesMap). Response body is the Rust Debug repr of the underlying ParseRequestError variant. The indexer's transform_lentgh_limit_exceeded middleware rewrites only the body-limit-overflow case to 413; all other 400s pass through unchanged. Note: GraphQL semantic/validation errors do NOT produce 400 — they return HTTP 200 with errors[] inside the response body.
fixes: Inspect the response body — it identifies the ParseRequestError variant in Debug format (e.g. 'InvalidRequest(...)', 'UnsupportedBatch')
Send well-formed JSON shaped as { "query": "...", "variables": {...}, "operationName": "..." }
Use Content-Type: application/json — the indexer does not expose multipart upload routes
Send one query per request; the single-request endpoint does not accept JSON-array batches

entries.name: PayloadTooLarge
entries.description: The request payload exceeds the indexer's maximum allowed size.
fixes: Reduce the size of the request payload
Paginate or split large queries

entries.name: ServiceUnavailable
entries.description: The indexer service is currently unavailable.
fixes: Wait for the indexer to finish syncing and retry
Check indexer health endpoint for status details

entries.description: Substrate transaction pool rejected the extrinsic as invalid. The wire-level error has the shape `{code: 1010, message: "Invalid Transaction", data: "Custom error: N"}` where N is the inner LedgerApiError u8. To decode N, look up codes 0-255 in this dataset.
fixes: Read the inner data string — it ends with `Custom error: N` where N is a u8 0-255
Look up that u8 in the Midnight node error tables (e.g. 168 was FeeCalculation, now retired; 155 is FeeCalculationError; 154 is BlockLimitExceededError)
If you only see `1010` with no inner u8, the cause is upstream substrate validation rather than ledger-level rejection — check signature, era, nonce

group.name: midnight-js-indexer-public-data-provider errors

entries.description: Abstract base class (added in v4.1.1) for all errors raised by the indexer public data provider. Catch with `instanceof IndexerError` to handle any indexer error; narrow to a subclass (IndexerFormattedError, IndexerQueryError, IndexerDataError, IndexerSubscriptionDataError, IndexerProviderConfigError) for specifics.

entries.name: IndexerFormattedError

group.name: midnight-js-indexer-public-data-provider errors
group.description: Errors from @midnight-ntwrk/midnight-js-indexer-public-data-provider.

entries.description: GraphQL errors received from the Midnight indexer, surfaced as a class-based error extending IndexerError. As of v4.1.1 the GraphQL error array is exposed via the `errors` field (readonly GraphQLFormattedError[]) — renamed from `cause` (BREAKING).
fixes: Inspect `errors` (readonly GraphQLFormattedError[]) for the underlying GraphQL errors — this field was named `cause` before v4.1.1

entries.name: IndexerQueryError

group.name: midnight-js-indexer-public-data-provider errors
group.description: Errors from @midnight-ntwrk/midnight-js-indexer-public-data-provider.

entries.description: Raised (v4.1.1+) when an Apollo query or fetch fails at the transport layer (network failure, malformed response, Apollo client error) — distinct from IndexerFormattedError. Preserves the original Apollo error via the standard `Error.cause`.

entries.name: IndexerDataError

group.name: midnight-js-indexer-public-data-provider errors
group.description: Errors from @midnight-ntwrk/midnight-js-indexer-public-data-provider.

entries.description: Raised (v4.1.1+) when indexer-returned data is structurally inconsistent with the provider's expectations. Carries a discriminated `context` (kind: 'unknown-status' | 'missing-contract-action' | 'missing-identifier'); construct via static factories unknownStatus/missingContractAction/missingIdentifier.
fixes: Branch on `error.context.kind`; usually an indexer/SDK schema mismatch or unindexed data — verify indexer compatibility and that the contract/transaction is fully indexed

entries.name: IndexerSubscriptionDataError

group.name: midnight-js-indexer-public-data-provider errors

fixes: Usually an indexer/SDK schema mismatch; verify indexer compatibility

entries.name: IndexerProviderConfigError

entries.description: Raised (v4.1.1+) when the consumer passes a configuration the provider does not support (e.g. an unservable observable mode). Signals API misuse, not a server-side issue.
fixes: Review the provider configuration / observable mode against the supported options

entries.name: CompactError

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Base error class for compact-runtime failures. Extended by typed assertion and type-error helpers.

entries.name: assert

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError with message 'failed assert: ${s}' when the boolean condition is false.
fixes: The assertion message ${s} describes the failed invariant; fix the inputs that violate it

entries.name: typeError

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError with message 'type error: ${who} ${what} at ${where}; expected value of type ${type} but received ${value}' (uses object-inspect for the received value).
fixes: Cross-check witness return types against contract-declared types

entries.name: Maximum field mismatch

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError with message 'Maximum field mismatch: compiled code uses ${MAX_FIELD}, runtime uses ${OCRT_MAX_FIELD}' when the field-modulus constant baked into the compiled code does not match the installed runtime's MAX_FIELD constant. Generated by runtime/export-version.ss into runtime/src/version.ts at runtime build time.
fixes: Recompile the contract with a compiler whose field modulus matches the installed @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime
Or install a runtime version whose MAX_FIELD matches what the compiler embedded

entries.name: convertFieldToBytes range error

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError with message 'range error at ${src}: Field or Uint value ${x} does not fit into ${n} bytes' when convertFieldToBytes is asked to encode a value that overflows the requested byte width.
fixes: Reduce the source value to fit in ${n} bytes
Cast to a wider Uint type that has enough byte width to hold the value

entries.name: convertBytesToField range error

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError with message 'range error at ${src}: byte vector [...] exceeds maximum value ${MAX_FIELD} of Field type' when convertBytesToField receives a byte sequence whose integer value exceeds the field modulus.
fixes: Ensure the source bytes encode a value below MAX_FIELD before casting to Field
Reduce or mask the input byte vector

entries.name: convertBytesToUint range error

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError with message 'range error at ${src}: byte vector [...] exceeds maximum value ${maxval} of target Uint type' when convertBytesToUint receives a byte sequence whose integer value exceeds the maxval of the target Uint width.
fixes: Cast to a wider Uint type whose maxval accommodates the value
Validate or clamp the input bytes before casting

entries.name: coerceToChargedState unexpected type

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError with message ''contractState' parameter ${contractState} has unexpected type' when createCircuitContext / createInitialQueryContext receives a contractState value that is not a ChargedState, ContractState, or StateValue.
fixes: Pass a value of type ChargedState, ContractState, or StateValue from @midnight-ntwrk/onchain-runtime-v1
Construct the state via the runtime's documented constructors instead of an ad-hoc object literal

entries.name: Onchain-runtime error wrapped as CompactError

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: The query-circuit wrapper in circuit-context.ts catches any Error from @midnight-ntwrk/onchain-runtime-v1 and rethrows it as new CompactError(err.toString()). The message text is whatever the upstream library produced — there is no fixed template. Look at the wrapped message itself for the underlying cause.
fixes: Read the wrapped message; it comes from @midnight-ntwrk/onchain-runtime-v1, not from compact-runtime
Search the onchain-runtime-v1 source or status-codes for that specific message

entries.name: expected JubjubPoint

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError('expected JubjubPoint') when CompactTypeJubjubPoint.fromValue receives a runtime value that does not contain the two field-element components of a Jubjub curve point.
fixes: Confirm the witness or upstream value really is a JubjubPoint (two Field components, x then y)

entries.name: expected MerkleTreeDigest

entries.name: expected Field

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError('expected Field') when CompactTypeField.fromValue receives a runtime value with no leading element to interpret as a Field.

Inspect witness or struct construction for an unexpected empty value at this position

entries.name: expected Enum

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError('expected Enum[<=${maxValue}]') when CompactTypeEnum.fromValue receives a runtime value with no leading element to interpret as an Enum value.
fixes: Confirm the upstream value contains the byte sequence that encodes the Enum tag
Inspect witness construction for an unexpected empty value at this position

entries.name: expected UnsignedInteger

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError('expected UnsignedInteger[<=${maxValue}]') when an Enum or UnsignedInteger value either has no leading runtime element to read or has a value exceeding the type's maxValue. Three distinct call sites share this template (Enum overflow + UnsignedInteger missing + UnsignedInteger overflow) — the message alone does not distinguish them; check the surrounding stack frame.
fixes: Confirm the upstream value is non-empty and within the target type's maxValue range
If overflow: cast to a wider UnsignedInteger or reduce the source value
If missing: inspect upstream value construction for an unexpected empty value

entries.name: expected N-element array

group.name: compact-runtime errors

entries.description: Throws CompactError('expected ${length}-element array') when CompactTypeVector.toValue receives an array whose length does not match the declared Vector length.
fixes: Pad or truncate the input array to the exact declared Vector length
Check witness return values for off-by-one length mismatches

entries.name: expected Boolean

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError('expected Boolean') when CompactTypeBoolean.fromValue receives a runtime value that is missing, has length > 1, or has a single byte that is not 0 or 1.
fixes: Confirm the upstream value is a single byte equal to 0 (false) or 1 (true)
Check witness return values that should produce booleans

entries.name: expected Bytes

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError('expected Bytes[${length}]') when CompactTypeBytes.fromValue receives a runtime value that is missing or has length greater than the declared Bytes length.
fixes: Confirm the upstream value is a Uint8Array no longer than the declared Bytes length
Truncate or re-encode the value to the correct length before producing it as a witness

entries.name: Expected but received

group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError('Expected ${expected} but received ${JSON.stringify(actual)}') from the expectedValueError helper used by type-guard assertions in contract-dependencies.ts. The exact ${expected} text identifies the asserted-against type (e.g., a ContractAddress, a CompactValue).
fixes: Inspect the JSON-stringified actual value to see what was passed
Match the actual value's shape to the expected type's invariants

entries.name: State map missing value

group.name: compact-runtime errors
group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError('State map ${stateMap} contains key without corresponding value') during compactADTDependencies traversal when a StateMap key has no associated value.

entries.name: State cast failed

group.description: Errors thrown by @midnight-ntwrk/compact-runtime at contract execution time.

entries.description: Throws CompactError('State ${stateValue} cannot be cast to a ${expectedCastOutput}') from assertCastSucceeded when a StateValue cannot be cast to the expected representation (map, array, or boundary Merkle tree).

entries.name: persistentCommit opening size
